featured.suggestedTitle: Urgent Updates: Chrome, Edge, and Oracle Identity Manager Vulnerabilities Patched
featured.suggestedSummary: Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge have released updates addressing 26 vulnerabilities, some critical. Concurrently, Oracle has patched a critical RCE flaw (CVE-2026-21992) in its Identity Manager. These vulnerabilities could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code or bypass security measures. Security teams should prioritize applying these updates to mitigate potential exploitation risks.

featured.reason: High-profile data breach by LAPSUS$ Group
featured.suggestedTitle: AstraZeneca Data Breach: LAPSUS$ Group Claims Internal Data Access
featured.suggestedSummary: The LAPSUS$ Group has allegedly breached AstraZeneca, accessing internal data. This incident underscores the persistent threat posed by sophisticated cybercriminal groups targeting large enterprises. Organizations should review access controls and monitor for indicators of compromise to prevent similar breaches.

quickNews.title: VoidStealer malware steals Chrome master key via debugger trick
quickNews.summary: An information stealer called VoidStealer uses a new approach to bypass Chrome's Application-Bound Encryption (ABE) and extract the master key for decrypting sensitive data stored in the browser. [...]
